doc_title: Draft METHOD 327 - Fugitive and Area Source Measurement of Selected Volatile Organic Hazardous Air Pollutants Using Specially Prepared Canisters

steps.text: Verify that the canisters used are at least 6 liters in volume and suitable for trace gas analysis.
Ensure the apparatus for characterizing the flow control device is properly assembled.
Conduct flow control verification testing for the sampling device, monitoring the canister pressure for 24 hours.
Verify the flow rate prior to and after each sampling event in the field.
Demonstrate that the sampling device is leak-free before sampling.
Test the sampling device for bias using zero-air and known-standard conditions.
Collect field duplicates using discrete mechanical flow control devices.
Collect canister field blanks by filling a canister with humidified clean diluent gas to approximately -15 in. Hg.
Prepare canisters for transport by shipping them in protective hard-shell boxes or sturdy cardboard boxes.
Analyze field samples using accurately calibrated instruments.
Perform leak checks prior to each sample analysis sequence.
Verify each sample canister connection as leak-free through the preconcentrator.
Measure the sample aliquot volume for analysis accurately.

preconditions.text: If canisters have been previously cleaned and stored under pressure, evacuate them prior to field deployment.
Before conducting any analyses, ensure that all equipment used meets performance criteria specified in the method.

constraints.text: Use specially prepared canisters that meet specific cleaning and performance criteria.
Maintain cleaning records for all canisters used.
Ensure that each target VOC's concentration must be < 20 pptv or undetected.
